perturbation.original_text: 1.2 "Interim Period" means a period of four (4) years commencing from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 "Interim Period" means a period of two (2) years commencing from the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement defines the Interim Period as four years. Changing this to two years introduces a contradiction because several clauses reference this period, especially concerning the licensing and use of trademarks. This change will create uncertainty regarding how long HERC can use the HERTZ trademarks, creating an in-text contradiction when reffering to the duration period in the clause 2.2.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HSI will retain ownership and will grant a royalty-free, non-exclusive license to HERC to use the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks (those foreign trademarks owned by HSI related to the ERB that incorporate the mark/name HERTZ) as set forth on Schedule A, for the Interim Period, outside the United States and Puerto Rico, as more fully set forth in the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement attached as Exhibit A. HERC shall immediately discontinue use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks upon expiration of the Interim Period, or the earlier termination of this Agreement or the Trademark, Trade Name, Domain and Related Rights License Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the 'Interim Period' as four years. The changed text shortens this to two years. The contradicted text grants a license to use trademarks 'for the Interim Period'. Because the 'Interim Period' is now shorter, the duration of the trademark license is also shorter. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency because the contradicted text relied on the original four-year period. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Non-Competition. During the Interim Period, neither HERC nor any of its affiliates or subsidiaries shall, directly or indirectly, engage in the business of renting or leasing cars, crossovers or light trucks (including sport utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles) in [any country in which THC or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries rents or leases cars, crossovers or light trucks (including sport utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles) as of the date of this Agreement] without THC's prior written consent, except to the extent materially consistent in type and scope with HERC's operations immediately prior to the date of this IPA. This provision shall survive the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Non-Competition. During the Interim Period, neither HERC nor any of its affiliates or subsidiaries shall, directly or indirectly, engage in the business of renting or leasing cars, crossovers or light trucks (including sport utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles) in [any country in which THC or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries rents or leases cars, crossovers or light trucks (including sport utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles) as of the date of this Agreement] without THC's prior written consent, except to the extent materially consistent in type and scope with HERC's operations immediately prior to the date of this IPA. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this agreement, HERC may, at its option, engage in said activities with a written notice no later than 10 business days. This provision shall survive the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a strict non-competition clause on HERC during the Interim Period, requiring THC's prior written consent to engage in certain business activities. By adding a sentence that allows HERC to engage in those activities with only a 10-day written notice, we create a direct contradiction. This undermines the need for prior consent and causes confusion. It introduces an in-text contradiction about the non-competition obligations by allowing HERC to ignore that specific term of the contract with a simple notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Competition. During the Interim Period, neither HERC nor any of its affiliates or subsidiaries shall, directly or indirectly, engage in the business of renting or leasing cars, crossovers or light trucks (including sport utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles) in [any country in which THC or any of its affiliates or subsidiaries rents or leases cars, crossovers or light trucks (including sport utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles) as of the date of this Agreement] without THC's prior written consent, except to the extent materially consistent in type and scope with HERC's operations immediately prior to the date of this IPA. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this agreement, HERC may, at its option, engage in said activities with a written notice no later than 10 business days. This provision shall survive the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction to the non-competition clause. The original text mandates THC's prior written consent for HERC to engage in certain business activities. The added sentence allows HERC to bypass this requirement with just a 10-day written notice, effectively negating the need for consent. This creates a conflict in the contract as it is unclear whether HERC needs consent or if a notice suffices, satisfying the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.
